assumptions: The intragroup dividend receivable/payable is a recognised monetary item denominated in a foreign currency.
The item remains recognised in the consolidated financial statements so that foreign exchange effects are not fully eliminated on consolidation.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value hedge accounting as a top-level hedge accounting model for recognised assets or liabilities exposed to changes in fair value attributable to a particular risk.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies cash flow hedge accounting as a top-level hedge accounting model for exposure to variability in cash flows attributable to a particular risk on a recognised asset or liability or a highly probable forecast transaction.

approaches.label: Hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 identify hedging of foreign currency risk arising from a net investment in a foreign operation as a distinct top-level hedge accounting model.

approaches.label: Foreign currency accounting without hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IAS 21 provides the standalone accounting model for exchange differences on foreign-currency monetary items when hedge accounting is not applied.
